what_it_does: A large CSS stylesheet for the Workshop module. It defines layout grids, component styles, a fixed-ratio slide canvas, a broad catalog of slide/presentation block patterns, widget and wizard builder UIs, context frames, AI candidate/diff/suggestion chrome, onboarding, shortcuts, and error-boundary styling. It relies on a warm gold/bronze/ivory custom-property theme.
observations: Monolithic file mixing slide canvas, builder UIs, AI chrome, and overlays under section comments.
Heavy reliance on CSS custom properties such as --accent-gold, --accent-bronze, --bg-surface, --fg-primary.
Slide canvas uses a fixed 1280x720 inner frame scaled from top-left to fit a 16:9 stage.

Many ws-blk-* block patterns: hero, headline, quote, stats, process, funnel, cluster, pullquote, metric row, bullets, iconstrip, labeledcard, comparison, hexbadges, chiprow, statpills, stattable, checklist, logostrip, bio, timeline, pillars, photobio, orgdiagram, contactcard, embed.
Color flips for dark and gold bleed motifs via [data-bleed] attribute selectors.
Widget builder (ws-wb-*) and wizard builder (ws-wz-*) sections with dashboard/hub/embed context frames.
AI engine UI classes: candidate gallery, skeleton shimmer, diff cards, suggestion rail, field tools.
Inline editable styling with .ws-editable and focused outline.
Slide thumbnails use transform: scale(0.125) to miniaturize the 1280px frame.
Decorative shapes use clip-path for hexagon, octagon, and diamond forms.
Later 'Polish pass' sections re-override earlier rules, creating some duplication.
standouts: Fixed-resolution slide canvas scaled via CSS to avoid reflow at narrow widths.
Extensive catalog of presentation block patterns with dark/gold motif flips.
Integrated AI candidate gallery with skeleton states, keyboard focus, and before/after diff cards.
Widget/wizard builder context frames (dashboard, hub, embed) sharing the same visual language.
conventions: BEM-ish naming with ws- prefix and -- modifiers.
CSS custom properties for color, spacing, radius, shadow, and easing tokens.
Grid and flex layouts with sticky side/rail panels.
Transitions and animations keyed to var(--ease-out).
concerns: Very long, monolithic stylesheet mixing many subsystems, increasing specificity conflicts and maintenance cost.
Several rules are redefined in later polish sections, creating duplication and override risk.
Timeline tick positions rely on nth-child selectors, which are fragile if step counts change.
Fixed pixel dimensions for slides and thumbnails may not adapt to all viewports.
Heavy use of color-mix and clip-path may have browser-support gaps.
Tooltip helper uses data-tip attribute with no visible accessibility hooks in this file.
notes: A sprawling, theme-driven CSS manifest for the Workshop design surface, covering slide blocks, builder UIs, AI assistant chrome, and polish overlays.
questions: Are the CSS custom properties it consumes defined in a separate root or theme file?
Is the slide scaling transform driven purely by CSS or adjusted dynamically by JS?
Which ws-blk-* block classes are actively rendered versus aspirational or stubbed?
Are the widget and wizard builder sections still fully active, or are some styles legacy?
Is the AI candidate gallery modal used across all content types or only specific ones?
purpose_vs_actual: The header says it extends Studio's design language for the Workshop canvas; the file does that but has expanded into a multi-subsystem stylesheet covering slides, builders, and AI chrome.
apparent_themes: Warm editorial gold/bronze/ivory luxury design language.
Builder/preview duality with live context frames.
AI-assisted creation through candidates, diffs, and suggestions.
intention_signals: It appears designed to unify slide/document composition, widget/wizard building, and AI-assisted editing inside a single warm-accented visual system, with strong emphasis on preview fidelity and generative assistance affordances.
novelty: The combination of a scaled fixed-resolution slide canvas, a large taxonomy of presentation block patterns with motif/bleed theming, and integrated AI candidate/diff/suggestion UI in one stylesheet.
contribution: It contributes a comprehensive set of reusable visual patterns and states for a document/slide builder, widget/wizard builder, and AI-assisted editing surface, all expressed through a shared warm-accent design-token vocabulary.
summary_for_embedding: app/workshop.css is a large theme-driven CSS file that styles the Workshop design canvas, including slide layouts, block content patterns, widget and wizard builder interfaces, AI candidate/diff/suggestion chrome, and onboarding/shortcuts overlays.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single shared visual language and component styling layer for the Workshop surface, enabling slide/document composition, embedded widget/wizard previews, and AI-assisted editing affordances.
